nodes.label: Unit 7: A Watering Rhyme / The Giving Tree
nodes.description: A poem about caring for plants and a story about selfless love

nodes.label: A Watering Rhyme (Poem)
nodes.description: A poem about the right way and time to water plants

nodes.label: Water in the Morning
nodes.description: Early morning is the best time to water plants, before the sun gets hot

nodes.label: Water in the Evening
nodes.description: Evening is also a good time, after the sun has gone down

nodes.label: Not at Noon!
nodes.description: Never water plants during the hot afternoon - the sun can burn wet leaves

nodes.label: Caring for Plants
nodes.description: Plants need regular water, sunlight, and love to grow strong and healthy

nodes.label: The Giving Tree (Story)
nodes.description: A story about a tree that gave everything to a boy it loved

nodes.label: Happy Childhood
nodes.description: As a child, the boy played, climbed, and rested under the tree every day

nodes.label: The Tree Keeps Giving
nodes.description: The tree gave apples, branches, and its trunk as the boy grew older

nodes.label: Only a Stump Left
nodes.description: In the end, the tree was just a stump but still offered a seat to the tired old man

nodes.label: Grammar: Tenses
nodes.description: Tenses tell us when an action happens - past, present, or future

nodes.label: Past Tense
nodes.description: Actions that already happened: played, climbed, gave, sat

nodes.label: Present Tense
nodes.description: Actions happening now: plays, climbs, gives, sits

nodes.label: Future Tense
nodes.description: Actions that will happen: will play, will climb, will give, will sit
